treatment_information.specific_treatment: subcranial and transnasal approach tumor resection
treatment_information.treatment_response: Mild postoperative diplopia resolved with time. No epiphora, cerebrospinal fluid leak, or vision loss was experienced.

treatment_information.dosage_or_details: 60 Gy to the tumor bed; 52.5 Gy to the bilateral neck

treatment_timeline.event: Initial biopsy of the mass in the clinic was non-diagnostic.
Endoscopic biopsy performed in the operating room.
Frozen sections taken from the posterior ethmoids revealed a small focus of carcinoma-in-situ (CIS) amid a large quantity of tissue consistent with VC.
Postoperative MRI was obtained after the biopsy to better characterize orbital, skull base, and brain involvement.
Tumor was resected through a subcranial and transnasal approach.
Patient received concomitant radiation and chemotherapy.
Repeat imaging and endoscopic exams showed him to be free of recurrence.

patient_outcome.follow_up_details: Repeat imaging and endoscopic exams showed him to be free of recurrence 11 months postsurgery, and he remains so 3.5 years postsurgery.
patient_outcome.complications: Mild postoperative diplopia resolved with time. No epiphora, cerebrospinal fluid leak, or vision loss was experienced.
